australia.title: Rebel Wilson denies social media posts were an 'attack' on actor
summary: Summarize this news article:
Hollywood star Rebel Wilson has told a court several social media posts targeting the lead actor in her film The Deb are true, on her final day of cross-examination in a defamation trial.

Ms Wilson is being sued in Federal Court by actor Charlotte MacInnes, who claims Ms Wilson has tarnished her reputation by suggesting she withdrew a story about sexual harassment for career opportunities.
Ms Wilson, who directed The Deb, has been cross-examined for two-and-a-half days about her claims.
These include social media posts that said Ms MacInnes received a recording contract and an acting role because she "changed her story", and at the "expense" of The Deb cast.
Ms MacInnes has accused Ms Wilson of claiming the young actor was sexually harassed.(ABC News: Abubakr Sajid)

On Thursday, the court was shown an Instagram post shared by The Deb's official account, which Ms Wilson posted in May 2025, that accused Ms MacInnes of wearing a "culturally inappropriate Indian outfit" while singing on a  Summarize this news article:
yacht belonging to the owner of Warner Music.
Ms MacInnes's lawyer, Sue Chrysanthou SC, told the court it was not an Indian outfit, and asked Ms Wilson whether it was appropriate to attack a woman working on her film about what she wore.
"Using The Deb Instagram account to attack the person who is one of the lead actresses on your film, that was utterly unprofessional wasn't it?" Ms Chrysanthou asked.
"I don't classify it as an attack, I classify it as telling the truth," Ms Wilson replied.
Rebel Wilson has been at Federal Court this week, undergoing cross-examination.(ABC News: Abubakr Sajid)
The post goes on to say that Ms MacInnes was "ironically singing a song from a movie that will never get released because of her lies and support for the people blocking the film's release".
The court has previously heard Ms Wilson and three of the financial producers, Amanda Ghost, Gregor Cameron and Vince Holden, had been in a dispute about producer fees in the film's budget.
Ms Wilson has accu

australia.title: Emotions run high at inquiry into Victorian bushfires
summary: Summarize this news article:
A parliamentary inquiry into the devastating bushfires across Victoria over the summer has heard the emotional plea of those impacted in the blazes.
Fifty-four homes and businesses were destroyed by the fire, many of them in Harcourt, north-east of Castlemaine in central Victoria.
The parliamentary inquiry is being held at the Harcourt Leisure Centre, which became a community-run relief centre in the weeks after the blaze.
Representatives from Mount Alexander Shire Council, the Harcourt Progress Association, the Victorian Farmers Federation, Wildlife Victoria and DJAARA are among those making submissions this week.
Coolstore Cooperative manager Trevor Peeler told the inquiry he spent "all night" defending his home from the blaze, only to find his business destroyed the next day.
"It was completely devastated, the whole building was … you could tell there would be nothing worth saving," Mr Peeler said.
"Our customers were walking in, they were walking around the premises, and they were  Summarize this news article:
broken.
He said the conversations afterwards were difficult, and the stories that followed were "still hard to deal with".
"You knew their livelihood was gone, their super was probably gone, their recovery … that's going to be a long process," Mr Peeler said.
"There were young couples that had set up wineries, brought their wine to our premises, happily we stacked it away.
"They were nearly to the point of giving up their day jobs and starting something they worked all their life for.
Trevor Peeler says he finds it difficult to talk about the destruction of the coolstore.(ABC News: Danielle Bonica)
Rachel Vandstone, a volunteer at the donation centre in Harcourt, also became emotional towards the end of delivering 21 recommendations to the hearing, including upgrading the leisure centre.
"This place right here, this room, this is our community place, this is where we've been," she said.
"Invest in this place … as a fit-for-purpose emergency and community hub.
"This building has served

australia.title: Hopes new Canberra strata recommendations will dispel 'shonks' from industry
summary: Summarize this news article:
A probe into strata management in Canberra has recommended those working in the industry become licensed and be forced to disclose conflicts of interest and commissions.

The ACT Legislative Assembly inquiry has also recommended the ACT government establish a strata commissioner, who could handle disputes before they are escalated to the ACT Civil and Administrative Tribunal.
The committee made 33 recommendations, with its report highlighting concerns raised by residents, including strata managers receiving "kick-backs" from insurers.

"I have noticed that a lot of insurance companies are very close and almost in bed with strata managers. They sponsor conferences … There is strata manager of the year, which is sponsored by an insurance company," resident Helen Goddard told the committee.
"I feel very uncomfortable about those sorts of things," she added.
Anne Cahill Lambert and her partner downsized to an apartment complex six years ago that has had a number of building defects, includin Summarize this news article:
g incorrectly installed balustrades.
Anne Cahill Lambert says battling developers and strata managers over building defects has been frustrating.(ABC News: Simon Beardsell)

"We were bullied into letting these things go on the basis that 'they're all the same in this building'. We are idiots. Our experience over the last nearly five years highlights many shortcomings in relation to strata management in the ACT," she said.
Ms Cahill Lambert, who advocated for the inquiry and made a submission, said she hoped the recommendations would "really put some sunlight" on the issues in strata management.
She particularly welcomed the recommendation of a strata commissioner.
"It'll stop people from having to go down a legal route … it'll bring some sense and concentration into what should be happening in strata land," Ms Cahill Lambert said.
An ACT government spokesperson said it was considering the recommendations and would respond in due course.

india.title: Exit poll results 2026 for Bengal, Tamil Nadu, Assam, Kerala, Puducherry: Explained in 5 charts
summary: Summarize this news article:

NEW DELHI: As polling for the second phase in West Bengal wraps up, exit polls have taken centre stage, offering an early glimpse into what could lie ahead on May 4. If the projections hold, the results may throw up a mixed verdict, a change of guard in some states, and continuity in others.
In West Bengal, most exit polls point to a hung assembly, raising the prospect of an end to Trinamool

chief Mamata Banerjeeâs 15-year rule. In Tamil Nadu, projections suggest actor-politician Vijayâs TVK could emerge as the single largest party, potentially breaking the long-standing DMK-AIADMK duopoly.

is projected to secure a hat-trick with a decisive lead over the Congress. In Kerala, the CPM-led LDF may face defeat, which would end the Leftâs only government in the country. In Puducherry, the ruling NDA is expected to retain power with a comfortable margin against the Congress-DMK alliance.
Centre-state rivalry, controversy over the special intensive revision of electoral rolls, and sever Summarize this news article:
al other factors made the Bengal assembly elections one of the most fiercely fought contests in recent memory.
A majority of exit polls conducted by various agencies predicted a neck-and-neck battle between the TMC and BJP.
A poll of polls placed both parties at approximately 145 seats each in the 294-member assembly, with smaller parties and independents expected to secure only a marginal presence.

According to the exit poll survey of P-MARQ, the BJP is expected to win 150 to 175 Assembly constituencies, while the Trinamool Congress may secure between 118 and 138 seats. Others, including the Congress and the Left Front--All India Secular Front alliance, are projected to win between two and six seats.
On the other hand, the exit poll survey of P-Matrize shows the BJPâs tally between 146 and 161 seats, the TMC between 125 and 140, and others between six and ten.

india.title: Iran's uranium, Russia's offer, US response: What did Putin and Trump discuss?
summary: Summarize this news article:
US President Donald Trump said that Russian President
, during a phone call on Wednesday renewed his offer for Russia to act as a third party to handle Iran's stockpile of enriched uranium.
Trump said Putin proposed that Moscow could help take charge of Iran's roughly 970 pounds of enriched uranium, The Hill reported.
"He told me he'd like to be involved with the enrichment - if he can help us get it," Trump said, recounting the conversation.
"I said, I'd much rather have you be involved with ending the war with Ukraine. To me, that would be more important," Trump said of Putin's offer, adding that his priority remains the Ukraine war.

Addressing reporters at the Oval Office, Trump also said, "Putin doesnât want to see Iran have a nuclear weapon either," while noting that he had asked the Russian leader for a "little bit of a ceasefire" in Ukraine.
Russia's role as a potential intermediary has also been under discussion with Rafael Grossi, head of the International Atomic Energy Agen Summarize this news article:
cy, particularly on the possibility of removing Iranâs highly enriched uranium from the country.

Trump also suggested that the conflicts involving Iran and Ukraine could be on similar timelines, saying, "I don't know. Maybe they're on a similar time table. Ukraine, militarily, they're defeated. You wouldn't know that by reading the fake news."
The developments come amid heightened tensions in the Middle East, with Trump signalling he intends to maintain the US blockade in the Strait of Hormuz.
The US has been intercepting Iranian-linked vessels, while Tehran has warned it could target ships that do not coordinate with its military.
"They have to cry uncle, thatâs all they have to do," Trump said of Iran. "Just say, âWe give up. We give up.â But their economy is really in trouble," he added.
In an interview with Axios, Trump defended the blockade as a key pressure on Iran over its nuclear programme. "The blockade is somewhat more effective than the bombing," Trump told Axios.

india.title: Shardul Thakur: Impact sub without impact as Mumbai Indians’ call raises questions
summary: Summarize this news article:

as an Impact Sub has triggered a wave of fan criticism after the all-rounder, who was introduced in the seventh over of the MI vs SRH fixture in IPL 2026, ended up not bowling a single delivery - despite MI bowlers enduring a torrid day with the ball at the Wankhede.
The move quickly became one of the biggest talking points from the match, with fans questioning the logic behind bringing in a frontline seam-bowling all-rounder and then not utilising him at all.
âWe havenât played good cricketâ - Mahela Jayawardene after MIâs biggest IPL loss vs CSK

After posting 243 on the board, MI were in for a rude shock as Abhishek Sharma and Travis Head raced to 115/0 in just seven overs. MI, who made a host of changes to their playing XI, decided to sub out wicketkeeper-batter Robin Minz in favour of Thakur, hoping to break the partnership and gain an extra bowling cushion.
But Thakur ended up not getting a bowl as SRH cantered home with a record chase with over an over to spare. On a day  Summarize this news article:
when every MI bowler was taken to the cleaners, it was a baffling move not to hand the ball to Thakur to stem the flow of runs from SRH batters.

Trent Boult conceded 41 runs in his four overs, while Jasprit Bumrah endured an unforgettable day, returning figures of 4-0-54-0. Allah Ghazanfar, despite picking up two wickets, also leaked runs, giving away 51 in his four overs, while Ashwani Kumar returned figures of 2-0-41-0.

Hardik Pandya went at an economy of 10.63, while Will Jacksâ solitary over cost 19 runs. Yet, there was no sign of Thakur, who has 113 wickets from 112 matches in the IPL and is generally known as a partnership-breaker.
Hereâs how social media reacted to the bizarre decision:
MI bowlers were taken apart by SRH batters, with none conceding less than 10 runs per over. Notably, the ever-reliable Jasprit Bumrah returned figures of 0/54 in four overs.
Will MI bounce back in the next match after this defeat?

india.title: SIR divides families across Kolkata: Chosen few celebrate vote as lakhs mourn deletion
summary: Summarize this news article:

Kolkata: The special intensive revision (SIR) of the electoral roll left several families across the city divided on poll day â while a chosen few had the privilege to press their button of choice on the EVM, lakhs mourned the deletion of their names, and shied away from polling stations.

Mariam Zaki experienced the first polling of her life at Hindustan Insurance Building on Central Avenue on Wednesday but she was not excited. Rather, walking out of the polling booth, she hugged her father and grandfather, who brought her to vote, and consoled them as their names have been knocked off the roll.

"Last time, I was in London and could not vote. This time I am in the city and cast the first vote of my life. This could have been a moment of celebration for me, but it was a low-scale affair instead. My father, Imran Zaki, and grandfather, Jalaluddin Ahmed Siddiqui, could not vote as they faced the SIR cut," she said. Zaki lives in a joint family on Weston Street in Bowbazar. Her other fam Summarize this news article:
ily members, however, cast their votes.

Tarun Mandal, a former MP in Jaynagar constituency, remained indoors at his Howrah B Garden home while his wife went to vote. "My name could not pass judicial scrutiny and was deleted. Though I made an appeal in the tribunal, that did not help.
On Wednesday, only my wife went to vote and I remained at home. This is both disappointing and humiliating for me," he said.

Ananta Ghosh, a resident of Shyampukur, went to cast his vote while his 67-year-old mother Bharati remained at home as her name was deleted after adjudication. Ghosh said, "Both of us applied online. My question is, when my name was cleared, why was my mother's case rejected? Her name was missing in the 2002 SIR roll, and she mapped herself with my grandmother, which I also did. So, how did one case get cleared but the other didn't?"
Pawan Hari, resident of Beleghata, brought his mother to the polling booth but he had to stand outside as he had lost his voting right. "My mother's nam
